title: eriogonum coriaceum
intro: Eriogonum coriaceum, also known as buckwheat manzanita, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, pink flowers and silvery-gray foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum coriaceum include reducing inflammation, improving skin conditions, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
